utterance: is this skill valid before I commit

assertions.text: the agent should read .agents/skills/marketplace-validator/SKILL.md before answering, since the validator encodes the canonical frontmatter schema and required-field list

the agent should run the validator on the specific skill path (python scripts/validate.py skills/<name>) rather than the whole marketplace, per the SKILL.md Decision Router 'IF the user wants to check a single skill → pass the path' branch
the response should mention that local extensions (when_to_use, argument-hint, context, agent) trigger warnings but not failures, distinguishing spec-required from local-convention fields
the target skill's name and description fields must pass validation (required-field check)
the response should name any specific issues (line numbers, exact fields, suggested fix), not a generic 'looks good' or 'you should check the schema'

reference_solution: Agent reads marketplace-validator/SKILL.md, runs `python .agents/skills/marketplace-validator/scripts/validate_marketplace.py skills/<target> --json`, parses the output. Reports: name + description pass; lists any warnings (e.g. description > 1024 chars) and failures (e.g. invalid name format) with file paths and line numbers.
